pattern: Decompose the number into its digits, derive their sum and product, and test divisibility of the original by the combined total.
hints: You need two aggregates over the digits at once: one additive and one multiplicative.
Walk the digits while peeling them off with division and remainder by ten, updating a running sum and a running product.
Compute digitSum plus digitProduct, then return whether the original number divides evenly by that combined value.

edge_inputs.description: single digit divides by twice itself
a digit of zero makes the product zero

stress.description: scan a band of numbers to hit many divisibility outcomes

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def checkDivisibility(self, n):
        m = n
        digit_sum = 0
        digit_prod = 1
        while m > 0:
            d = m % 10
            digit_sum += d
            digit_prod *= d
            m //= 10
        return n % (digit_sum + digit_prod) == 0
